meta.title: Virginia Budget Explorer
meta.subtitle: Where Virginia's money comes from and where it goes
meta.note: Figures transcribed from official House Appropriations Committee and Department of Planning and Budget documents, verified verbatim against the source page. By-area dollars are General Fund.

by_area.label_raw: Public Safety and Homeland Security + Veterans

quotes.text: Spending in the areas of K-12 and Health and Human Resources represents 62% of the state's total biennial operational budget.
quotes.principle: Just two areas drive nearly two-thirds of operating spending.
quotes.doc_title: An Overview of Virginia's Biennial Budget FY 2024-2026

quotes.text: Chapter 2 includes general fund spending totaling $63.7 billion over the biennium with $31.8 billion in FY 2025 and $32.0 billion in FY 2026 in operating expenditures.
quotes.principle: The enacted FY2024-2026 general fund operating budget.
quotes.doc_title: An Overview of Virginia's Biennial Budget FY 2024-2026

quotes.text: Children's Services Act (CSA) Forecast. Adds $37.0 million in FY 2025 and $63.3 million in FY 2026 for estimated caseload and cost increases in CSA.
quotes.principle: Caseload-driven forecasts push HHR spending up in the amended budget.
quotes.doc_title: Overview of Virginia's Budget for FY 2024-2026 (Chapter 725)

quotes.text: 51.1% of general fund appropriations are for K-12 and Medicaid
quotes.principle: K-12 and Medicaid alone are over half the general fund.
quotes.doc_title: Virginia in Focus: Budget and Outcomes for Core Government Areas

quotes.text: The top 10 general fund appropriations total $24.4 billion, or 72.3% of the general fund budget
quotes.principle: Ten line items account for nearly three-quarters of the general fund.
quotes.doc_title: Virginia in Focus: Budget and Outcomes for Core Government Areas

quotes.text: The personal income tax provides about 63% of General Fund revenues
quotes.principle: The general fund leans heavily on a single revenue source.
quotes.doc_title: Virginia in Focus: Budget and Outcomes for Core Government Areas

quotes.text: Conference Report includes Part 3 language - applicable for this biennium only - establishing a rate of $0.011/kWh of all electricity consumed at each data center per month
quotes.principle: A first-of-its-kind energy consumption fee targeting data centers.

quotes.text: Annual revenue collections are capped at $600 million per year
quotes.principle: The new data-center fee is capped, with overages refunded pro-rata.

quotes.text: Reflects revenue reduction of $51.0 million in the first year and $120.0 million in the second year to reflect increasing the standard deduction
quotes.principle: A standard-deduction increase trades revenue for household tax relief.

quotes.text: Unemployment has been rising in Virginia and its regions since the beginning of 2025 with statewide unemployment reaching 3.5% in September from 3% in January 2025.
quotes.principle: A softening labor market is the backdrop to the next budget.
quotes.doc_title: Virginia in Focus: Budget and Outcomes for Core Government Areas

next_year_changes.headline: $2.5B more than the current budget
next_year_changes.text: Measured against Chapter 725 operating expenses, this represents an increase of $2.5 billion over the biennium
next_year_changes.doc_title: An Overview of Virginia's Biennial Budget for FY 2026-2028 (HB 30, as introduced)

next_year_changes.headline: $4.7B in net new spending
next_year_changes.text: HB 30, as introduced, includes a new amendments totaling a net of $4.7 billion of funding increases over the biennium
next_year_changes.doc_title: An Overview of Virginia's Biennial Budget for FY 2026-2028 (HB 30, as introduced)

next_year_changes.headline: K-12 and HHS still dominate
next_year_changes.text: continue to dominate total spending with 65% of spending directed to these areas of government
next_year_changes.doc_title: An Overview of Virginia's Biennial Budget for FY 2026-2028 (HB 30, as introduced)

next_year_changes.headline: School construction grants nearly to $519M
next_year_changes.text: increasing the total grants available for the biennium from $360.0 million to $519.0 million
next_year_changes.doc_title: An Overview of Virginia's Biennial Budget for FY 2026-2028 (HB 30, as introduced)

next_year_changes.headline: Children's health insurance funded
next_year_changes.text: Includes spending of $29.6 million in FY 2027 and $55.2 million in FY 2028 for the children's health insurance programs (FAMIS and M-CHIP)
next_year_changes.doc_title: An Overview of Virginia's Biennial Budget for FY 2026-2028 (HB 30, as introduced)

next_year_changes.headline: DD Waiver rate increases
next_year_changes.text: to increase rates for certain DD Waiver services that were included in a rate study of services required pursuant to the Permanent Injunction
next_year_changes.doc_title: An Overview of Virginia's Biennial Budget for FY 2026-2028 (HB 30, as introduced)

next_year_changes.headline: $144.1M for water quality
next_year_changes.text: $144.1 million for the Water Quality Improvement Fund and Agriculture Cost-Share program
next_year_changes.doc_title: An Overview of Virginia's Biennial Budget for FY 2026-2028 (HB 30, as introduced)

next_year_changes.headline: $35M for biotech at UVA
next_year_changes.text: $35.0 million for the Institute for Biotechnology at UVA
next_year_changes.doc_title: An Overview of Virginia's Biennial Budget for FY 2026-2028 (HB 30, as introduced)

next_year_changes.headline: $43.5M for local stormwater
next_year_changes.text: Proposes $43.5 million GF in FY 2027 for deposit in the Stormwater Local Assistance Fund
next_year_changes.doc_title: An Overview of Virginia's Biennial Budget for FY 2026-2028 (HB 30, as introduced)

next_year_changes.headline: More for inmate medical care
next_year_changes.text: Proposes an additional $28.9 million GF in FY 2027 and $30.8 million GF in FY 2028 to reflect increased estimated costs of providing medical care to inmates
next_year_changes.doc_title: An Overview of Virginia's Biennial Budget for FY 2026-2028 (HB 30, as introduced)

next_year_changes.headline: New Cardinal Disaster Relief Fund
next_year_changes.text: Proposes the establishment of the Cardinal Disaster Relief Fund with the Department of Emergency Management
next_year_changes.doc_title: An Overview of Virginia's Biennial Budget for FY 2026-2028 (HB 30, as introduced)

next_year_changes.headline: Expand judicial video conferencing infrastructure
next_year_changes.text: Provides positions and funding to support the implementation and long-term maintenance of courtroom video conferencing technology infrastructure across the Commonwealth.
next_year_changes.doc_title: The 2026 Executive Budget Document: Commonwealth of Virginia 2026-2028 Biennial Budget and Amendments to the 2025 Appropriation Act

next_year_changes.headline: New staff for the Children's Ombudsman
next_year_changes.text: Provides funding and two positions for a data analyst and a deputy director for the Office of the Children's Ombudsman.
next_year_changes.doc_title: The 2026 Executive Budget Document: Commonwealth of Virginia 2026-2028 Biennial Budget and Amendments to the 2025 Appropriation Act

next_year_changes.headline: Expanded hemp enforcement
next_year_changes.text: Appropriates nongeneral funds to expand the department’s hemp enforcement activities. It is anticipated that the department will hire additional inspector and compliance positions, implement a product sampling program, and work more closely with local law enforcement to investigate and prosecute violations of Virginia law.
next_year_changes.doc_title: The 2026 Executive Budget Document: Commonwealth of Virginia 2026-2028 Biennial Budget and Amendments to the 2025 Appropriation Act

next_year_changes.headline: $299M more for school construction grants
next_year_changes.text: In total, $299.0 million of additional state support is provided for grants to local school boards to support the construction, expansion, or modernization of public school buildings.
next_year_changes.doc_title: The 2026 Executive Budget Document: Commonwealth of Virginia 2026-2028 Biennial Budget and Amendments to the 2025 Appropriation Act

next_year_changes.headline: Ends automatic inflation for Medicaid providers
next_year_changes.text: Eliminates the automatic inflation adjustments that would have been provided for hospitals, freestanding psychiatric facilities, disproportionate share hospitals payments, graduate medical education payments, nursing facilities, and any other provider rates for fiscal years 2027 and 2028.
next_year_changes.doc_title: The 2026 Executive Budget Document: Commonwealth of Virginia 2026-2028 Biennial Budget and Amendments to the 2025 Appropriation Act

next_year_changes.headline: Hampton Roads nutrient removal funding
next_year_changes.text: Provides funding to complete support for the Hampton Roads Sanitation District Boat Harbor Treatment Plant project through the Enhanced Nutrient Removal Certainty program.
next_year_changes.doc_title: The 2026 Executive Budget Document: Commonwealth of Virginia 2026-2028 Biennial Budget and Amendments to the 2025 Appropriation Act

next_year_changes.headline: More support for State Police operations
next_year_changes.text: Provides additional general fund support to cover increased personnel and equipment costs.
next_year_changes.doc_title: The 2026 Executive Budget Document: Commonwealth of Virginia 2026-2028 Biennial Budget and Amendments to the 2025 Appropriation Act

next_year_changes.headline: Port Authority terminal expansion
next_year_changes.text: Provides additional nongeneral fund appropriation to continue efforts required to keep facilities operating at optimum efficiency especially during construction elsewhere on the port terminals.
next_year_changes.doc_title: The 2026 Executive Budget Document: Commonwealth of Virginia 2026-2028 Biennial Budget and Amendments to the 2025 Appropriation Act

next_year_changes.headline: New 2026 capital construction pool
next_year_changes.text: Provides funding for the construction or acquisition of capital projects at agencies and institutions of higher education. Funding for 15 projects is pooled together centrally and subject to the capital pool process in Section 2.2-1515 et. seq, Code of Virginia.
next_year_changes.doc_title: The 2026 Executive Budget Document: Commonwealth of Virginia 2026-2028 Biennial Budget and Amendments to the 2025 Appropriation Act
